items.title: Man charged with attempted murder over Wynyard shooting of 37-year-old man
items.summary: The man allegedly responsible for a shooting in Tasmania's north west on Wednesday has been charged with attempted murder.

items.title: Universities warn against Coalition plan to cut foreign student numbers in migration crackdown
items.summary: Universities are bracing for foreign student numbers to absorb the bulk of cuts under a Coalition migration plan, with experts warning skilled visas would be difficult to cull.

items.title: Speeding driver who struck and killed novelist Brenda Walker jailed for four years
items.summary: Lucas Martins Da Silva was driving his hatchback more than 30 kilometres an hour over the speed limit when he struck Brenda Walker in Perth's CBD.

items.title: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping meet at Zhongnanhai Garden
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Socceroos star Mo Toure says he went with a hunch by moving to Norwich City
items.summary: Rising Socceroos striker Mo Toure says a "gut feeling" was the reason behind his wildly successful move to the English Championship.

items.title: Former Victorian premier Daniel Andrews to be immortalised with statue
items.summary: The government says work is already underway to honour the state's longest-serving Labor premier.

items.title: Coalition tax plan could cost more than suggested in first few years
items.summary: The Coalition's plan to index tax brackets could cost $35 billion in the first four years, more than has been suggested, and $44 billion in one year alone in 2035-36.

items.title: How to kill off your lawn and plant a native garden instead
items.summary: Many of us are looking to create a native garden as a lower maintenance, more sustainable and often cheaper alternative.

items.title: ABC boss Hugh Marks flags TV program cuts as broadcaster pivots to new platforms
items.summary: The ABC's managing director says the public broadcaster's biggest challenge is "stopping things" as he seeks to grow audiences on new platforms without increasing staff.

items.title: Four students in hospital after suspected drug 'overdose' at Central Coast high school
items.summary: Fifteen students at a Central Coast high school have been assessed by paramedics, with four being taken to hospital.

items.title: Body found in Tarneit believed to be of man kidnapped during South Morang home invasion, police say
items.summary: A man's body was found near a children's playground in Melbourne's west after a violent home invasion in the city's north.

items.title: Liberty Bell Bay smelter workers get another two weeks pay reprieve
items.summary: Workers at Australia's only manganese smelter will be thrown an additional lifeline as the state and federal governments step in with a two-week pay offer. It comes days before a previous short-term deal was due to expire.

items.title: Sydney and Fremantle lead early flag chase, but dual styles make Hawthorn a strong chance
items.summary: An early review of the AFL season so far points to a split between two distinct styles, with one side making a case for being flag favourites by showing it can deliver both.

items.title: Push to overturn South East fracking ban to be blocked by One Nation, Liberals
items.summary: A controversial plan to end a ban on fracking in South Australia's South East two years earlier than planned, appears to be all but dead, with the Liberals and One Nation vowing to oppose legislation.

items.title: Eight people arrested at North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment site, police say
items.summary: Police say eight people have been arrested at the North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment site this morning.

items.title: Flight with Australian and New Zealand passengers from hantavirus cruise ship arrives in Perth
items.summary: A flight carrying six people who were on board the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship lands at an air force base in Perth.

items.title: The federal government wants to use AI to speed up drug and housing approvals. Can we trust it?
items.summary: This week’s budget revealed a number of ways the federal government is expected to use artificial intelligence to cut back on red tape. One expert says the benefits are "mostly good".

items.title: Sydney doctor Andrew Small charged with alleged sexual assault of patients
items.summary: Sydney GP Andrew Small is granted bail, after being charged with allegedly sexually assaulting multiple female patients at an inner-city medical centre.

items.title: Carlton interim coach Josh Fraser not ready to take full-time role after Michael Voss exit
items.summary: Josh Fraser will lead Carlton for the remainder of the season after Michael Voss's exit, but isn't prepared to do the job full-time just yet.

items.title: Federal Court rejects northern cattle industry's appeal over impact of live export ban
items.summary: Australia's northern cattle industry says it is "deeply disappointed" by a Federal Court decision to dismiss its appeal over compensation for the 2011 live export ban.

items.title: Cocaine hidden in fruit pulp found by Australian Federal Police at Melbourne Airport
items.summary: Police say criminal syndicates are becoming more creative at concealing drugs within airport shipments.

items.title: Culture, gender and birth order meant I would always be in the 'sandwich generation'
items.summary: As the eldest daughter in a Vietnamese family, it was an unspoken expectation I would be the one to care for my aging parents.

items.title: Calls for drought declaration as southern Queensland graziers begin to destock
items.summary: Graziers start destocking to preserve dwindling feed with rainfall well below average across the region.

items.title: Taiwan conducts military drills before Trump visits China
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Northern Territory records Australia's first diphtheria death reported in almost a decade amid outbreak
items.summary: The Northern Territory has recorded the country's first reported death from diphtheria in almost a decade, as experts urge people to get vaccinated.

items.title: Israeli nationalists march through Jerusalem Old City to mark annexation of East Jerusalem
items.summary: Thousands of Israeli nationalists, including young boys, join crowds chanting death threats towards Palestinians while marching through Jerusalem Old City.

items.title: Angus Taylor's budget reply shows times have changed since Peter Dutton
items.summary: To appreciate how much times have changed, it is worth comparing the opposition leader's budget reply speech to Peter Dutton's.

items.title: Matthew Wale voted in as new Solomon Islands prime minister after secret ballot
items.summary: Matthew Wale is the new prime minister of Solomon Islands after winning a vote in the Pacific island nation's parliament.

items.title: National Commissioner for Indigenous children says people chosen to lead NT child protection review won't 'cut it'
items.summary: The National Commissioner for Indigenous children has criticised the selection of a retired NSW police commissioner and long-standing NT public servant as the leaders of a review into the territory's child protection system.

items.title: Australia vs Ireland AFLW clash locked in for North Sydney Oval in August
items.summary: Australia and Ireland will meet in an international women's match under Australian rules to kick off the 2026 AFLW season.

items.title: Manjimup's migrant community unite to mourn deaths of Vanuatu farm workers in horror crash
items.summary: Residents of a WA country town have come together to mourn the deaths of two Pacific island farm workers as investigations into their deaths continue.

items.title: Driver dies after car rolls over onto railway line at Kulangoor
items.summary: A 28-year-old man has died after a serious crash on the Sunshine Coast this morning.

items.title: Vaccine program almost halves number of young babies hospitalised with RSV
items.summary: A new immunisation program for pregnant mothers and newborns has almost halved the number of young babies being hospitalised with a serious respiratory virus, according to a new report.

items.title: Police charge teens over arson attempt at Bar Bambi in Melbourne
items.summary: Police allege Bar Bambi in ACDC Lane has again been targeted by arsonists, while officers also investigate suspicious truck fires at two businesses in Melbourne's inner-west.

items.title: Victorian country firefighters slam bureaucracy for slashing roadside burns by 90pc
items.summary: Victorian fire authorities release new findings that show how planned burns helped control and contain the devastating summer bushfires.

items.title: Activist, 78, arrested at North Adelaide Golf Course, as protesters chain themselves to machinery
items.summary: An elderly activist has been arrested after breaking through the gates of the North Adelaide Golf Course, while protesters earlier this morning claimed they had chained themselves to machinery in condemnation of hundreds of trees being cut.

items.title: Budget designed to help young people criticised as still favouring baby boomers
items.summary: The prime minister says this budget is about fixing intergenerational inequality, but online, gen Z and the millennials are hearing a different narrative.

items.title: Melanie Lynskey pays tribute to the women driving the Pike River Mine disaster campaign
items.summary: Standing side by side with real-life heroes Anna Osborne and Sonya Rockhouse, the Yellow Jackets star is a passionate ally for their cause.

items.title: WA Premier Roger Cook backs federal budget housing focus
items.summary: Roger Cook welcomes the federal budget's focus on housing, but he's less than thrilled about NDIS changes and the funding of infrastructure and aged care.

items.title: Man faces court charged with murdering elderly mother in her Canberra home
items.summary: Marcus Kauffman made a brief appearance in the ACT Magistrates Court this morning, where no bail application was made.

items.title: US vote to halt Iran war narrowly defeated as Israel and Lebanon have 'productive' first day of talks
items.summary: Israel and Lebanon have reported a productive first day of talks in Washington as the deadline for the ceasefire between the two countries approaches.

items.title: Min Woo Lee takes share of clubhouse lead at PGA Championship, Scotty Scheffler ominously poised
items.summary: Australian Min Woo Lee enjoys a share of the first-round clubhouse lead, but Masters champ Rory McIlroy has endured a rough start to the PGA Championship.

items.title: Wildlife rescuers say more fur seals are becoming entangled in fishing gear
items.summary: Data from the wildlife rescue group ORRCA shows an increase in the number of fur seal entanglements as they seek out fishing lines in attempts to find an easy meal.

items.title: Seals found entangled in fishing line or rope on the rise
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: ASX closes lower, oil price up as Trump and Xi discuss war in Iran — as it happened
items.summary: The ASX200 finished the week lower, and oil headed for a weekly gain as the reopening of the critical Strait of Hormuz waterway and end to the war in the Middle East remain in doubt.

items.title: Mount Isa homes to be sold after Indigenous organisation fails to pay rates
items.summary: Tenants in some Mount Isa homes have endured years without repairs, hot water or proper leases and now one corporation's failures could leave them homeless.

items.title: Champions League race overshadowed as Serie A serves up fixture chaos thanks to Italian Open
items.summary: The race for the Champions League spots has been overshadowed as Serie A serves up fixture chaos relating to the Italian Open tennis tournament in Rome.

items.title: Delta Goodrem makes Eurovision 2026 grand final after performing pop ballad Eclipse
items.summary: Delta Goodrem will perform in the Eurovision 2026 grand final for Australia after a rousing performance of the song Eclipse during the semifinal.

items.title: Music program takes regional artists from the Northern Territory to UK for The Great Escape festival
items.summary: Northern Territory musicians attending The Great Escape music festival in the UK are hoping their experience will open more doors for regional artists.

items.title: Seismic testing linked by research to decreased fish catch numbers in Gippsland
items.summary: New research links seismic testing used for oil and gas exploration with decreases in commercial fish catch rates in Victoria's east.

items.title: Archaeologists restore colonial-era boat found in Barangaroo
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Residents feel impact of no permanent GP in Strahan since 2017
items.summary: Jamie hopes his small town's almost decade-long run without a permanent GP is about to end, so he will not have to keep repeating his story to every doctor who passes through.

items.title: South East SA locals voice outrage at proposal to lift fracking ban
items.summary: Producers on South Australia's Limestone Coast say they are stunned by a government proposal to lift the fracking ban in the region.

items.title: Energy Australia's bid to build 1.4-gigawatt gas-fired power plant at Marulan
items.summary: Energy Australia says the proposed 1.4-gigawatt gas-fired power plant would be critical to the energy transition by supplying reliable back-up power, but analysts have flagged supply and economic issues.

items.title: Australian government rejects clean-up deadline for Antarctica's Wilkes Station
items.summary: The Australian government has officially ruled out cleaning up Antarctica's contaminated Wilkes Station before the end of the decade, as previously recommended by a federal parliamentary committee.

items.title: ABC weekly news quiz: A Eurovision boycott, a pungent frog, a mysterious red globule and some impressive Neanderthals
items.summary: This is not the week to boycott the news quiz, when a particularly ripe frog and a big, red … something … await you.

items.title: Liberal Party mistakenly emails branch about 'deceased member'
items.summary: When John Ternel quit the Victorian Liberal Party this week the party mistakenly sent an email to members saying he had died. Mr Ternel says the ordeal confirmed to him that the Victorian Liberals are in shambles.

items.title: Magic Round crowds told to pack raincoats ahead of patchy Queensland showers
items.summary: A trough in the west and onshore winds in the east will bring showers to parts of Queensland, however the driest parts are set to miss out.

items.title: Chinese Australians lash Coalition proposal to block non-citizens from welfare services
items.summary: Chinese Australians have lashed the Coalition's plan to block non-citizens from accessing welfare services, likening the opposition's targeting of migrants to One Nation.

items.title: Tens of thousands of rugby league fans descend on Brisbane's Lang Park for blockbuster Magic Round
items.summary: Brisbane's Lang Park plays host to the NRL's biggest weekend, with 16 teams competing in eight matches, plus game two of the NRLW State of Origin.

items.title: Modernising Queensland veterinary act essential for future of industry, experts say
items.summary: Experts says veterinary technologists and nurses are being under-utilised in a sector they say is in "crisis."

items.title: High-income earners use discretionary trusts to pay less tax, but will budget change have unintended consequences?
items.summary: The federal budget includes a new 30 per cent minimum tax on discretionary trusts. Experts say it is not just the ultra-wealthy that will be hit.

items.title: TasInsure unravelling a lesson for Tasmanian Liberals in promise management
items.summary: In a week where "broken promise" is being shouted by the Tasmanian Liberals in support of their federal colleagues' attack on the Labor budget, closer to home, a promise of their own is giving them grief.

items.title: How the wisdom of experience won the State of Origin series for New South Wales
items.summary: New South Wales series win over Queensland proves that sometimes, like anything else, practice makes perfect in State of Origin football.

items.title: Ambulance Victoria data shows 59 overdose call-outs at Crown Melbourne in two years
items.summary: Paramedics have attended more than 50 suspected overdoses at Crown Melbourne in the past two years, data released exclusively to the ABC shows.

items.title: Labor and the Liberals have staked their fortunes on duelling visions for income taxes
items.summary: Labor and the Liberals are offering imperfect but undeniably bold alternative visions for the tax system. It's nice to have something to talk about.

items.title: Coalition will repeal Labor's property tax changes and spend $22b on income tax cuts
items.summary: The Coalition would repeal all of Labor's negative gearing and capital gains changes and spend $22.5 billion over four years handing back tax "bracket creep" to workers if elected.
